segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with eerie, suspenseful music featuring a low drone and high-pitched ringing, accompanying a dark, shadowy close-up of a person's back with hair tied up, setting a tense atmosphere. The scene transitions to a dimly lit, rustic interior where a young woman, later identified as Anya, in a blue tunic stands solemnly holding a staff. A man in dark leather approaches her with concern, gently touching her face and embracing her, as he asks, "Your name is?" and she replies, "Anya," followed by shared laughter. He then gives her a dangerous task, stating, "I have something for you, and it has to be packed very carefully... it can poke him full of holes if you're quick enough." Anya confidently asserts, "I can be quick," as he instructs her, "You have to work at it every day. First lesson. Stick him with the point end." She responds, "I know which end to use." The music intensifies as the setting shifts to an outdoor grassy field where a young boy in a brown tunic is engaged in a sword fight, accompanied by clashing sounds and an aggressive male voice shouting, "I've got you, you little c*nt!" The boy falls after being struck, while a distressed female voice repeatedly cries out, "Anya! No, Anya!" The mood then shifts to a somber, mournful tone as the scene cuts to a warmly lit dining area. Here, Anya, now dressed in black with braided hair, is seated at a table, holding a knife with a focused or distressed expression. She states, "I'm practicing," to which another, older female voice asks, "Practicing for what? The Prince?" The second voice then shouts, "Anya, stop! He's a liar and a coward, and he'll kill..." before the segment ends.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with an intimate, dimly lit scene where the Man in Dark Leather, with long dark hair and a beard, gently touches the face of the Young Woman in Blue Tunic, who has braided hair. He speaks with a calm, authoritative voice, reminding her, "You are a Stark of Winterfell. You know our words," to which she softly replies, "Winter's coming." He continues, "You were born in the long summer... But now winter is truly coming, and in the winter, we must protect ourselves. Look after one another." The scene then shifts to a bright, sunlit outdoor courtyard where the Young Woman, now appearing more mature and resolute, confronts the Curly-Haired Man, who holds a staff. She questions him, "Who are you?", and he dramatically reveals in a distinct, accented voice, "Your dancing master, Syrio Forel." This is followed by a blurred, fast-moving shot and intense sounds of sword and staff fighting, grunts, and impacts, suggesting a fierce battle. The Curly-Haired Man's voice then shifts to a commanding tone, telling her, "You will marry a high lord and rule this castle. And your sons shall be knights." The Young Woman defiantly responds, "No. That's not me." The scene cuts to the Man in Dark Leather, now in a formal tunic, seated beside the Young Woman, who wears a light tunic, as they seriously discuss a large bundle of parchment. The Curly-Haired Man's intense voice returns, asking, "Do you pray to the gods?", to which she replies, "The old and the new." He then declares with profound conviction, "There is only one god. And his name is Death." The final moments return to a close-up of the Young Woman, her expression tense and worried, as she is touched on the shoulder by the Man in Dark Leather, before cutting back to the Curly-Haired Man, his face contorted with emotion, concluding with his powerful statement, "And there is only one thing we say to Death. Not today."

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a close-up of a fearful and distressed young woman in a light-colored tunic and scarf, standing in a dimly lit, stone-walled interior, accompanied by a knight in ornate dark armor. A deep male voice ominously asks, "What do we say to the God of death?" to which the young woman, with a clear, resolute voice, replies, "Not today." The scene shifts to a tense conversation between the young woman and a group of armored men, including a man with long hair and a beard in dark leather, as a male voice commands "Go," amidst sounds of shouting and chaos. The setting transitions to a bright, open outdoor area where a flock of black birds flies across a hazy sky, followed by a close-up of the young woman, now with wet, disheveled hair and a resolute expression, as intense, dramatic orchestral music swells, punctuated by a sharp, metallic clang. She is then seen in a forest with a man in a brown tunic, who speaks to her urgently. The young woman, identifying herself with a slightly higher-pitched but firm voice, states, "It's Arya. I've passed out." The man in the brown tunic responds, "You're a highborn then, you're a lady," to which the young woman replies, "No. I mean, yes. My mother was a lady, and my sister." The man continues, "You were a lord's daughter, and you lived in a castle. I should be calling you 'my lady'," but the young woman firmly states, "Do not call me 'my lady'." The man replies, "As my lady commands," then adds, "That was our lady like." A violent moment ensues as a man in a brown tunic is struck with a sharp impact and falls to the ground with a grunt and a thud near a cauldron, while another man in a green tunic reacts in shock. The video then moves to a dimly lit interior, possibly a cave or stone room, where a bearded man in dark clothing speaks seriously to a young boy with blonde hair, who listens intently, asking in a calm, thoughtful male voice, "How do you sleep when you have those things in your head?" A different, gruff male voice exclaims, "You didn't see that!" and continues, "I made damn sure." The young boy, with a soft, slightly hesitant voice, replies, "I close my eyes and I see them up there," adding, "All of them." The final frames show a dark, chaotic outdoor scene with a man in dark clothing being thrown or falling violently amidst shouts, grunts, and sounds of struggle, concluding with a sharp impact, suggesting a confrontation or attack.

segments.integrated_caption: The sequence opens in a dark, wooded area with the ominous sound of a blade being drawn, as a bald man in dark, checkered armor stands over a seemingly injured young boy in a brown tunic. The bald man's male voice chillingly remarks, "That's a fine little blade," and later suggests, "Maybe I'll pick my teeth with it." Amidst this, a young female voice, belonging to the young woman, is heard whispering names like "Joffrey," "Cersei," and "Ilyn Payne," hinting at a list of grievances. The scene then shifts to a dimly lit stone interior where a man with long, graying hair and a beard, dressed in dark leather armor, is seated, exuding authority. His deep, stern voice commands, "Bring the girl," adding, "I need a new cupbearer." A young woman with short, dark hair, wearing a rough, dark tunic, stands before him, her expression tense and defiant. A knight, clad in ornate armor, interjects with "My Lord." The man in dark leather then interrogates the young woman, asking, "And what do they say of Robb Stark in the North?" She replies with her young, clear voice, "They call him the Young Wolf," and asserts, "They say he can't be killed." When pressed, "Do you believe them?", she defiantly states, "No, my Lord. Anyone can be killed." The camera cuts to a close-up of the man's cold gaze before the young woman turns to face the knight, who later speaks with a distinct male voice, telling her, "You saved me and the two I was with. You stole three deaths from the Red God. We have to give them back. Speak three names, and the man will do the rest." The young woman and the knight are then seen peering through a gap in an outdoor stone wall, the knight's hand resting on her shoulder in a protective gesture. The final shots show the young woman alone, her face hardened by flickering firelight, before the knight reappears with a concerned look. Her young voice then shouts, "Amory Lorch!", to which the knight's voice confirms, "The girl has named a second," intensifying the atmosphere of betrayal, survival, and impending conflict.

segments.integrated_caption: The sequence opens in a dimly lit, shadowy environment where a young woman with short, dark hair, dressed in a rough, dark tunic, appears distressed and weary. She is in close proximity to a knight with long, wavy hair clad in dark, studded armor, who speaks to her with a serious, paternal demeanor, placing a protective hand on her shoulder as they hide behind a stone wall. The knight instructs her, saying, "Remember what we do with must be done. Now!" The scene then shifts to a darker, enclosed space, possibly a cave or dungeon, where the young woman stands resolute, her expression hardened, amidst the sounds of a struggle. A group of armored men, including one with long, graying hair and dark leather armor, move through the space with torches, creating a tense and foreboding atmosphere illuminated by flickering firelight. A man with short, dark hair and a rugged tunic, possibly a leader or strategist, is shown in close-up, his face lit by the flames, exuding determination and focus. The knight's voice continues, instructing, "If the day comes when you must find me again, just give that coin to any men from Braavos and say these words to him. Valar Morghulis." The young woman, her eyes wide with concern, repeats, "Valar Morghulis." As the man in the rugged tunic turns his head with grim resolve, sounds of a dog barking and growling are heard, followed by a man exclaiming, "Aha! Not a man at all! A HOUND!" A gruff voice then shouts, "Girl! What in seven hells are you doing with this Stark bitch?" The young woman, her resolute expression now fueled by anger, defiantly confronts the unseen speaker, accusing, "You murdered Myca! The butcher's boy! My friend! He was 12 years old! He was unarmed! And you rode him down!" Amidst the tense exchange, the man in the rugged tunic states, "I'm done serving," to which the young woman quickly retorts, "You just said you were serving Lord Beric." He continues, explaining, "He may be their leader, but they chose him. They're family," and adds with a hint of sadness, "I've never had a family." The young woman, perhaps offering comfort, says, "I can be your family," but the man dismisses her, "You wouldn't be my family." The consistently low and dramatic lighting, with torches and fire providing the only illumination, casts deep shadows and highlights the characters' intense emotions throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The sequence opens in a dark, rain-soaked forest with the sounds of a violent struggle, grunts, and sword clangs as a man in dark leather armor stands over a fallen figure. The scene shifts to a dimly lit interior where a young woman in black, her hands stained with blood, holds a small, round object. A bald man in checkered armor observes her grimly, asking with a direct tone, "Is that the first man you've killed?" to which she resolutely replies, "The first man." As she stands over an injured man on the ground, looking down with sorrow and resolve, a male antagonist's voice offers a trade: "One of our little chickens for one of yours. Give us a go at your friend." Amidst more sounds of struggle, another male voice taunts the injured man about his leg, who stutters a confused reply. The young woman then asks the injured man, "Can you walk?" before menacingly remarking about a "fine little blade" she might "pick my teeth with." The setting changes to an overcast outdoor landscape where a knight in ornate armor confronts the young woman. Distressed, she cries out, "He took us in! He fed us!" The knight, with a deep, authoritative voice, dismisses her, stating, "He's weak. He can't protect himself. We'll both be dead come winter. That man don't need silver." The young woman defiantly shouts back, "You're the worst shit in the Seven Kingdoms!" The knight calmly retorts, "I just understand the way things are. How many Starks they got to behead before you figure it out?" He then turns and walks away into the misty distance, leaving her alone, his voice echoing with a final, dismissive challenge: "Go on, do it for your supposed friend. Dead like all the rest of your friends."

segments.integrated_caption: The sequence opens with intense, percussive music and sounds of struggle as a man in dark, studded armor, the Knight in Ornate Armor, stands over the motionless Young Woman in Black, raising a dagger. His grim, conflicted expression is underscored by his deep, gravelly voice declaring, "Your friend's dead. But Meram Trant's not. Just John Tadama. And a big fucking sword." The camera cuts to a close-up of the Young Woman in Black’s face, her eyes wide with pain and fear as the dagger hovers. The scene shifts to a misty, wooded area where the Young Woman in Black, now sitting, tends to an older man, the Man in Rugged Tunic, who is injured and bleeding. Her soft, strained but determined voice states, "Nothing could be worse than this," to which the Man in Rugged Tunic, with a weary, philosophical tone, replies, "Maybe nothing is worse than this." She continues, "Nothing isn't better or worse than anything," and "Nothing is just nothing," before angrily recounting, "He told me he'd fuck me bloody with a stick." The Man in Rugged Tunic, with a calm, cynical voice, observes, "This day's really not working out the way you planned. He on your little list?" She defiantly responds, "He can't be. I don't know his name." He then asks, "What's your name?" and she reveals, "Rogue." He replies, "Thank you." The atmosphere remains tense and somber, with muted colors and foggy lighting. Intense, percussive sounds and metal clanging return as the Man in Rugged Tunic encourages her, "You're learning." The scene transitions to a wider shot of Rogue and the Knight in Ornate Armor standing together in the mist, both looking off with solemn expressions, the Knight placing a hand on her shoulder in a gesture of camaraderie. Rogue then urgently calls out, "Come with me, Arya," revealing the Man in Rugged Tunic's name. The final shot reveals a broader battlefield, littered with bodies, as Rogue and the Knight in Ornate Armor walk through the carnage with resolute faces, before cutting to a new figure, a Woman in Dark Armor, standing alone in a desolate, mountainous landscape with an expression of shock or realization.

segments.integrated_caption: The sequence opens with a close-up of the Young Woman in Black, her face etched with sorrow and resolve, standing amidst a bleak, fog-laden landscape. A harsh male voice questions, "Safety! Where the fuck's that?", listing the deaths of her aunt, mother, father, and brother, and declaring "Winterfell's a pile of rubble," before concluding, "There's no safety, you dumb bitch." A ghostly overlay of a bearded man’s face, possibly a memory or vision, appears as the Young Woman in Black is then seen lying injured on rocky ground, her hand reaching out to the Man in Rugged Tunic, who is also wounded and bleeding from his arm, with a grim, cold atmosphere and mist swirling around them. A metallic clanking sound precedes a desperate female voice pleading, "Kill me! Kill me!" The scene transitions to a warmer, sunlit setting where the Curly-Haired Man, now in a rugged tunic, confronts the Young Woman in Black with intense emotion, gesturing urgently. He asks, "How do you?", to which she calmly replies, "Valar Morghulis," and he responds with "Valar Dohaeris." The Young Woman in Black then confronts him, accusing, "You said there was no Jaqen H'ghar here," to which he cryptically replies, "There isn't. A man is not Jaqen H'ghar." She presses, "Well, who are you then?", and he states, "No one. And that is what we go must become." The mood shifts to a dim, stone-walled interior where the Young Woman in Black stands before a hooded figure—the Knight in Ornate Armor—holding a staff, his expression stern and authoritative. The Curly-Haired Man's voice continues, philosophically stating, "H'ghar wants to serve herself. Yeah, we serve the Many-Faced God, to serve well. H'ghar must become no one." The Young Woman in Black then walks through a dark, torch-lit corridor, her face illuminated by flickering light, her expression hardened with determination. The Curly-Haired Man's voice then asks, "Whose sword is that?", before revealing, "It belongs to Arya Stark. Arya Stark." The final shot reveals the Woman in Dark Armor standing alone in a desolate, mountainous landscape, her polished dark armor gleaming faintly in the dim light, her eyes wide with shock or realization, suggesting she has just witnessed or discovered something pivotal. Throughout, the lighting shifts from cold, misty blues to warm, golden tones and back to deep shadows, emphasizing emotional and narrative transitions.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a tense confrontation in a rugged, rocky outdoor setting, where the Curly-Haired Man, speaking with a deep, questioning tone, addresses the Young Woman in Black as "Caswall." He challenges her, asking, "You think anyone will dare tell you women aren't allowed?" The Young Woman in Black, initially distressed, responds with a determined "I can't" and firmly states, "I'm not going back north." A wider shot reveals the Red-Haired Woman in Fur-Collared Cloak and the Young Boy in Fur-Lined Cloak observing the intense exchange with concern. The focus returns to Caswall, who, with growing resolve, asks the Curly-Haired Man, "Where are you going? What's west of Westros?" When he admits, "I don't know," she declares, "No one knows. It's where all the maps stop. That's where I'm going." A somber, orchestral score underscores the dramatic dialogue. The scene then abruptly transitions to a dimly lit interior, where a silhouetted figure in dark clothing walks through a shadowy corridor towards a brightly lit doorway. The camera follows this figure before cutting to Caswall, now standing alone on a ship’s deck, gazing out at the vast, gray sea under a cloudy sky. The melancholic music swells as she is shown from different angles, her posture resolute, emphasizing her solitary determination as the camera pans across the imposing dragon-headed prow of the ship, symbolizing her journey into the unknown.
